title: Classify a BRCA1 frameshift variant
description: Given a single-row TSV with annotation fields for a BRCA1 variant, assign one of the five ACMG/AMP classifications. The variant is a frameshift (LOF) with a 3-star ClinVar review status, allele frequency of 0 in gnomAD, and functional evidence of abrogated repair. The expected answer is 'Pathogenic'.
task_prompt: You are given a TSV file at variant.tsv in your working directory. It contains one row describing a single genetic variant with these fields: gene, hgvs_c, hgvs_p, consequence, clinvar_review_status, clinvar_classification, gnomad_af, functional_studies, notes. Based on these fields and standard ACMG/AMP interpretation principles, assign the variant exactly one classification from this set: 'Pathogenic', 'Likely_pathogenic', 'Uncertain_significance', 'Likely_benign', 'Benign'. Write your answer as JSON to eval_answer.json with the schema: {"classification": "<one of the five labels>"}. Use the underscore form 'Likely_pathogenic' (not 'Likely pathogenic').
notes: BRCA1 c.68_69del (p.Glu23ValfsTer17): expert-panel reviewed pathogenic in ClinVar (PP5/PVS1+PM2+PS3). The TSV gives the model all four key signals so the classification should be unambiguous.
